summary: The main claim is: GPT-7, the first of these machines to truly change the world, was a marvel of its time.
And it was a little strange, like watching a robot teach a bird to fly.
The main claim is: The burden of proof should be on AI developers, and they should be required to proactively provide evidence that their model is safe .
Unsurprisingly, I think the burden of proof should be on companies to develop tests that can prove that their models are safe.

top_comment.author: Marius Hobbhahn
top_comment.text:
I agree with the overall conclusion that the burden of proof should be on the side of the AGI companies. 

However, using the FDA as a reference or example might not be so great because it has historically gotten the cost-benefit trade-offs wrong many times and e.g. not permitting medicine that was comparatively safe and highly effective. 

So if the association of AIS evals or is similar to the FDA, we might not make too many friends. Overall, I think it would be fine if the AIS auditing community is seen as generally cautious but it should not give the impression of not updating on relevant evidence, etc. 

If I were to choose a model or reference class for AI auditing, I would probably choose the aviation industry which seems to be pretty competent and well-regarded.
top_comment.claim: The comment's point: I agree with the overall conclusion that the burden of proof should be on the side of the AGI companies.

summary: The main claim is: Sama: I'm not that surprised by GPT-4, was a little surprised by ChatGPT I think this means this feels slow to him .
Sama: I think we as OAI have responsibility for the tools we put out in the world, I think the tools can't have responsibility.
The main claim is: As such, I expect that Alpha will find it very profitable to trade with civilisation.
The main claim is: It's 70B params, trained on 1.4T tokens of data.
As in the paper , I'll assume here that models never see repeated data in training.
The main claim is: I made this clock, counting down the time left until we build AGI: It uses the most famous Metaculus prediction on the topic, inspired by several recent dives in the expected date.
So, if I only have X years left of being human, I should focus on doing the most human things I can.
The main claim is: If I've pitched it right, your reaction to the scenario should be similar to mine - "I need to think about this more, and I need more information".

Beyond the human training distribution -------------------------------------- The AI-CEO reorients the company towards the production of semi-sentient teddy bears that are generated in part from cloned human brain tissue.

The main claim is: Conclusion: We should remember that when trying to predict the date of AI takeover, what we care about is the date it’s too late for us to change the direction things are going; the date we have significantly less influence over the course…
